search_queries_used: site:entnet.org hoarseness dysphonia guideline update key action statements
site:nice.org.uk persistent hoarseness suspected cancer referral
site:doh.gov.ae voice complaint guideline Abu Dhabi
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
The current guideline cautions against empirical anti-reflux treatment for isolated dysphonia.
The 2018 update replaces the older edition.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for dysphonia and hoarseness across ages; not exact for every sore-throat, dysphagia, haemoptysis, otalgia, or laryngitis cause.
setting_applicability: Exact for primary and otolaryngology assessment principles; UAE referral pathways require local review.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE voice guideline was identified; AAO-HNSF guidance requires UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The current AAO-HNSF 2018 update page and official 2026 laryngeal-examination measure were opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer published AAO-HNSF dysphonia guideline update was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped voice-change, hoarseness, loss-of-voice, cough or URTI, reflux context, voice use, tobacco, duration, voice observation, and terminology fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped persistent symptoms, respiratory distress, neck mass, smoking, laryngeal or neck assessment, referral, and worsening fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented voice-care education, management discussion, questions, follow-up, and outcome review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

unresolved_source_gaps: Throat discomfort, sore throat, dysphagia, haemoptysis, ear pain, broad throat or ear examination, prior-record review, fixed two-week timing, and empirical reflux-management wording remain unsupported.
Laryngitis remains clinician-stated; isolated dysphonia must not trigger empirical reflux, antibiotic, or steroid treatment.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
